title: lotus cytisoides subsp. prostratus
intro: Lotus cytisoides subsp. prostratus, also known as the creeping lotus or prostrate lotus, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family, commonly found in the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid and saponin content. The health benefits of Lotus cytisoides subsp. prostratus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and can also be dried and used as a powder or capsule supplement.
